threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Reapply mix gains when destinations materialize**

When `destination(forMixer:bus:)` is nil during attach, this new no-fallback path leaves the mixer masters at unity and does not apply `dryGain`/`wetGain` anywhe
threads.verdict_notes: Added EffectNode.refreshMixState() (default no-op), overridden by ReverbNode/EQNode to call applyMixGains(); Graph.refreshNodeMixState() is now called after engine.start() in powerOn and reattach. On-device verification of the preset-bypass case pending.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Update ADRs for changed tap muting semantics**

This changes the runtime tap behavior to `.mutedWhenTapped`, but ADR-014 is still Accepted and says `createTap` sets `.muted`, while ADR-018 says the tap mute behav
threads.verdict_notes: The code's .mutedWhenTapped is intentional (EXP-027: .muted makes AudioDeviceStart fail on the direct-IOProc path). Fixed by amending the stale ADRs, not the code: ADR-014 (Status + new Amendment section + alternative + references) and ADR-018 (decision step 1, consequences, references).

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Avoid touching the engine input node**

This diagnostic read violates the capture-v2/ADR-018 invariant that the engine's `inputNode` is never touched; on the macOS 26 path documented there, input-node involvement
threads.verdict_notes: Removed the engine.inputNode.outputFormat read from the [EXP-029.engine.preattach] diagnostic; kept the outputNode read. Added a NOTE comment citing ADR-018/EXP-023 so it is not reintroduced.

threads.finding_excerpt:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Do not clean up active resources from another instance**

The orphan cleanup matches aggregates only by the global `tap-n-filter.aggregate.` UID prefix and later destroys every match, with no check that the ownin
threads.verdict_notes: Logged as U-012 in docs/decisions/uncertainty-log.md, deferred to V0.2. Real multi-instance hazard, but tap-n-filter is single-instance for V0.1 and the disableOrphanCleanup escape hatch exists. V0.2 adds a PID/liveness ownership marker.

threads.finding_excerpt:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🌐 Web query:

`Does accessing AVAudioEngine.inputNode lazily instantiate and connect the default input device on macOS?`

💡 Result:

No—accessing AVAudioEngine.inputNode does not, by itself, instantiate
threads.verdict_notes: Same line/issue as the Codex thread on the inputNode read (PRRT_kwDOSjmLjM6Flbqp). Fixed by removing the access in 05d77c1.

threads.finding_excerpt:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Doc comment names the wrong HAL property.**

This comment says the list is fetched via `kAudioHardwarePropertyProcessObjectList`, but the implementation (line 505) correctly queries `kAudioHardwarePropertyTapList`. `kAudioHardwarePropertyProcessObj
threads.verdict_notes: Corrected the enumerateProcessTaps() protocol doc comment to name kAudioHardwarePropertyTapList (the impl at line ~505 already uses it). Phrasing tightened slightly vs the suggested diff.

threads.finding_excerpt:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Gate the reader test on idle capture state.**

`runReaderTest()` guards only `isReaderTestRunning` and `currentSource`. If invoked while production capture is `.running`, this spins up a second tap + aggregate device on the same process. That devic
threads.verdict_notes: Added `guard captureState == .idle` (with a warning log) to runReaderTest(), using the existing Equatable CaptureState, so the debug reader test cannot create a second tap during live capture.

threads.finding_excerpt:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Comment contradicts behavior: a single 4096-frame read per tick under-drains at 48 kHz.**

A 100 ms window at 48 kHz produces ~4800 frames/channel, so one `frameBatch` (4096) read per cadence cannot keep up — the ring overflows and `totalFrames` is
threads.verdict_notes: performReaderTest now drains the ring fully each tick (inner while-loop) instead of a single 4096-frame read; comment updated. Debug-only diagnostic path.
